  :                            173 
  :                            174 
  :                            175 .	the other 3 functions process two 192-bit inputs
  :                            176 
  :                            177 .	but 192-bit divide must test 
  :                            178 .	whether the divisor is represented in 1 or 2 floating numbers
  :                            179 
  :                            180 .	a 96-bit number sounds big
  :                            181 .	but if the divisor exponent is < 0 or > 31 decimal equivalent
  :                            182 .	then the 2nd divisor floating number is nonzero
  :                            183 .	for now deliver an indicative result in this case
  :                            184 .	by rounding both dividend and divisor to 96 bits
  :                            185 .	(adding their major and minor parts) and delivering a 96-bit quotient
  :                            186 
  :                            187 .	if the divisor is only one floating number (minor part is zero)
  :                            188 .	deliver a 192-bit quotient from a 192-bit dividend 
  :                            189 
  :                            190 fp_divide
00:00513F CFF004              +191 	dl	right+4
00:005140 B80004              +192 	xorB	a		. divisor longer than 96 bits?
00:005141 AE0151              +193 	jpb	fp_divide_again	. no, the minor divisor number is not normalised
  :                            194 
  :                            195 	$if	1
00:005142 8FF004              +196 	c	(reciprocal192)	right,,float192
00:005143 8FF004              +196 
00:005144 FCF008              +196 
00:005145 FF0F77              +196 
00:005146 38000F              +196 
00:005147 47F000              +197 	qs	right
00:005148 4F0008              +198 	ql	$residue
00:005149 47F004              +199 	qs	right+4
  :                            200 fp_long_divide
00:00514A FCF000              +201 	lc	heavy	left,,i	right,,i
00:00514B FCF009              +201 
00:00514C FCF002              +201 
00:00514D 3E00B1              +201 
00:00514E 38000F              +201 
00:00514F 1E014A              +202 	jdr	fp_long_divide
  :                            203 
  :                            204 	$else
  :                            205 	ql	left		. yes, the minor divisor number is normalised
  :                            206 	fa	left+4		. no possibility of sensible 192-bit quotient
  :                            207 	qs	left		. round the dividend to 96 bits
  :                            208 	dsa	48		. clear minor dividend
  :                            209 	ds	left+4
  :                            210 	ds	left+6
  :                            211 	ql	right		. round the divisor to 96 bits
  :                            212 	fa	right+4
  :                            213 	qs	right
  :                            214 	ql	left
  :                            215 
  :                            216 fp_short_divide			. calculate a 96-bit quotient
  :                            217 	fd	right
  :                            218 	jdr	fp_short_divide
  :                            219 	qs	left
  :                            220 	$endif

  :                            173 	$if	1
  :                            174 				. integer divide instruction executed
  :                            175 				. simply and once gives 48-bit signed
  :                            176 				. quotient and 24-bit signed remainder
  :                            177 				. from 48-bit dividend and 24-bit divisor
  :                            178 
  :                            179 				. quotient high order bits are in register 6
  :                            180 				. quotient low-order bits are in a
  :                            181 				. remainder is in b
  :                            182 
  :                            183 				. this sequence produces 72-bit signed
  :                            184 				. quotient from 72-bit dividend
  :                            185 i_divide
  :                            186 i_divide_again
  :                            187 
00:0053A4 CFF008              +188 	dl	left+1		. read first 48 dividend bits
00:0053A5 F0F006              +189 	d	right+3		. only 24 divisor bits can be used
  :                            190 				. with integer divide instruction
  :                            191 				. for more than that use floating point
  :                            192 				. or program a bit-race routine
  :                            193 
00:0053A6 280007              +194 	sb	7		. set aside the first remainder
00:0053A7 200005              +195 	sa	b		. reassemble the first 48-bit quotient

00:0053A9 C7F008              +197 	ds	left+1		. store the upper 48 quotient bits

  :                            410 .	here is a large difference between

  :                            418 .	when you subsequently store the same
  :                            419 .	managed object, you write new data
  :                            420 .	at the address in the descriptor
  :                            421 .	and the rules initially stored apply
  :                            422

  :                            464 
00:0054E4 D50004              +465 	ana, xi	4
00:0054E5 7E04EB              +466 	jc	mib_store_long_range?
00:0054E6 B604F1              +467 	j	mib_store_range?
  :                            468 
  :                            469 .	the method of having signed and unsigned range checks
  :                            470 .	without noticing too much
  :                            471 
  :                            472 .	firstly, if the high-order bit of the upper and lower limit
  :                            473 .	are identical, just do unsigned compares, and that will give
  :                            474 .	the right result whether the candidate integer is meant to
  :                            475 .	be signed or not
  :                            476 
  :                            477 .	secondly, if the high order bits of the two limits are 
  :                            478 .	different, then
  :                            479 
  :                            480 .	if the upper limit interpreted as unsigned is greater than
  :                            481 .	the lower limit interpreted as unsigned (the high order bit
  :                            482 .	of the upper limit is on, and not the high order bit of the
  :                            483 .	lower limit), just do an unsigned compare, because the
  :                            484 .	low-high difference cannot be a negative difference between
  :                            485 .	two signed integers. It can only be a lower limit in the
  :                            486 .	lower half of the number space, and an upper limit in the
  :                            487 .	upper half of the unsigned number space
  :                            488 
  :                            489 .	in all the above cases, the candidate integer is within
  :                            490 .	the range tested, if both carry is generated from the
  :                            491 .	subtraction of the lower limit from the integer, and also
  :                            492 .	carry is generated from the subtraction of the integer from
  :                            493 .	the upper limit
  :                            494 
  :                            495 .	this depends upon carry being consistent with adding the
  :                            496 .	minus of one number to the plus of another
  :                            497 
  :                            498 .	RTA1 is logically correct for this. The result and carried
  :                            499 .	result of
  :                            500 
  :                            501 .		ana	THIS
  :                            502 .		anb	THAT
  :                            503 .		dan	THE_OTHER
  :                            504 
  :                            505 
  :                            506 .	are identical to the result and carried result of
  :                            507 
  :                            508 .		aa	MINUS_THIS
  :                            509 .		ab	MINUS_THAT
  :                            510 .		da	MINUS_THE_OTHER
  :                            511 
  :                            512 .	carry$ set after ana anb dan means the unsigned number in
  :                            513 .	the accumulators was at least as large as the number
  :                            514 .	subtracted from it
  :                            515 .		
  :                            516 .
  :                            517 .	if the high-order bit of the lower limit is on and not the
  :                            518 .	high order bit of the upper limit, this is a signed range
  :                            519 .	which includes the value zero between its upper and lower
  :                            520 .	bounds. Then the candidate integer is within the range
  :                            521 .	tested if both the result of the integer minus the lower limit
  :                            522 .	is of positive polarity, and also the result of the upper limit
  :                            523 .	minus the integer is positive polarity
  :                            524 
  :                            525 .	polarity (and zero) are tested in the registers holding the
  :                            526 .	results, because the only condition flag in RTA1 is carry$
  :                            527 
  :                            528 .	examples of instructions testing results other than carry are
  :                            529 
  :                            530 .		jpa	here
  :                            531 .		jnb	there
  :                            532 .		jdz	everywhere
  :                            533 .		jzb	b_is_zero
  :                            534 .		jpe	a_has_even_parity
  :                            535 
  :                            536 .		tp	location_a_few_million
  :                            537 .		j	the_word_at_location_a_few_million_is_negative
  :                            538 
  :                            539 .		tz	2, sp
  :                            540 .		j	the_third_register_in_the_internal_stack_is_nonzero
  :                            541 
  :                            542 
  :                            543 mib_store_long_range!

00:005612 680004              +122 	anu, xi	SNMP_STRING
00:005613 DD0004              +122 
00:005614 DE0686              +123 	jnzb	a2_drop		. there must be a community string
(+03)0f
00:005615 2021F5              +124 	sa	a2_ber_transient(3):ojet
  :                            125 	
  :                            126 
00:005616 60000C              +127 	la	p
(+03)0f
00:005617 2021F7              +128 	sa	a2_ber_transient(3):data
(+03)10
00:005618 5D21F5              +129 	ly, xi	a2_ber_transient(3)
00:005619 3E07EB              +130 	lcal	ber_rs
  :                            131 
  :                            132 
  :                            133 
  :                            134 .	the parts to pull out are
  :                            135 .	_________________________
  :                            136 
  :                            137 .	30LL	binding and length		00..7F 81:80..FF 82:0100..FFFF
  :                            138 .	0201vv	SNMP version	00 = v 1
  :                            139 
  :                            140 .	then for v 1 at least...
  :                            141 
  :                            142 .	04LLalpha community string
  :                            143 
  :                            144 .	AxLL	command byte pdu length		00..7F 81:80..FF 82:0100..FFFF
  :                            145 .	160 = get 161 = getnext 163 = set
  :                            146 
  :                            147 .	0204xxxxxxxx	request ID typically 4 bytes
  :                            148 
  :                            149 .	020100	status
  :                            150 .	020100	error index
  :                            151 
  :                            152 .	30LL	binding and length		00..7F 81:80..FF 82:0100..FFFF
  :                            153 .	30LL-2	maybe another one nested
  :                            154 
  :                            155 .	06LLxxxxxx...0500	oids with NULLtype zeroL for get / getnext
  :                            156 .	06LLxxxxxx...4103yyyyyy	oids with typeLL / value for set
  :                            157 
  :                            158 .	strings may be > 127 bytes, length is encoded
  :                            159 .						00..7F 81:80..FF 82:0100..FFFF
  :                            160 
  :                            161 .	______________________________________________________________
  :                            162 
  :                            163 .	if the command is not A0 A1 A3 the request is dropped silently
  :                            164 
  :                            165 .	if the bindings lengths don't figure with the containing lengths
  :                            166 .	the request is dropped silently
  :                            167 
  :                            168 .	if community string is unknown the request is dropped silently
  :                            169 
  :                            170 
  :                            171 .	the response is as the request except
  :                            172 .	_____________________________________
  :                            173 
  :                            174 .	the command byte or response is SNMP_RESPONSE A2 162
  :                            175 
  :                            176 .	the response to successful get / getnext contains
  :                            177 .	a value encoding, not NULLtype/zeroL 0500
  :                            178 
  :                            179 
  :                            180 .	if anything has went wrong
  :                            181 
  :                            182 .		status is the error
  :                            183 
  :                            184 .		1 = something was too large in some way		tooBig
  :                            185 .		2 = that does not exist				noSuchName
  :                            186 .		3 = value wrong type or out of range		badValue
  :                            187 .		4 = object may not be wrote			readOnly
  :                            188 .		5 = something else was wrong			genErr
  :                            189 
  :                            190 .		error index indicates the [1st] oid binding which is in error
  :                            191 .		relative 1, 1 = 1st oid binding in the PDU
  :                            192 
  :                            193 .	the plan is
  :                            194 .	take all the actions and table the results as BER templates
  :                            195 
  :                            196 .	if anything is wrong, send the bindings back as received
  :                            197 .	with error-code set to the mistake
  :                            198 .	and error-index pointing to the first binding which is in error
  :                            199 
  :                            200 .	(no further ones need be scanned if that happens)
  :                            201 .	this means the update values are returned but not actioned
  :                            202 
  :                            203 .	and the read-requests are returned with null values as received
  :                            204 
  :                            205 .	even multiple get-nexts can be sensibly answered on this principle
  :                            206 
  :                            207 .	and if everything has went right, produce the BER-encoded results
  :                            208 
  :                            209
